articles.title: How a War Can Change Your Money and Bills
articles.summary: A war between the US, Israel, and Iran is affecting money and bills in the UK. Petrol prices have gone up a lot, making it more expensive to drive cars. This can also make food and other things cost more because transport gets pricier. Mortgage rates, which are the costs for borrowing money to buy a house, have also increased instead of going down as people hoped. The war makes oil prices rise, and when oil is expensive, many things become more costly. People are advised to drive less and save fuel to help with these changes. How long this lasts depends on how long the war goes on and how fast things can get back to normal.

articles.title: NASA Sends Two Spacecraft to Learn About Mars' Missing Air
articles.summary: Mars used to be warm and wet with lots of air, but now it is cold and dry with very little air. Scientists think the solar wind, which is a stream of particles from the Sun, blew away Mars' atmosphere over billions of years. NASA launched two spacecraft called ESCAPADE on November 13, 2025, to study how this happened. These twin spacecraft will work together around Mars to watch how the solar wind interacts with the planet's magnetic field, helping scientists understand why Mars lost its air. This mission will also help protect future astronauts who might visit Mars by learning about space weather. The spacecraft started their instruments on February 25 and will collect data as they travel to Mars and once they arrive.

articles.title: Curiosity Rover Finds Spiderweb Shapes on Mars
articles.summary: NASA's Curiosity rover is exploring Mars and found strange spiderweb-like ridges on the ground. These ridges are tall and crisscross for miles, making patterns that look like giant webs from space. Scientists think they formed long ago when water flowed underground through cracks in the rock. The water left minerals that hardened into ridges, while the softer rock around them wore away. This discovery is exciting because it shows water might have been on Mars longer than we thought, which could mean tiny life might have survived there billions of years ago. The rover is carefully driving on these ridges to study them up close and learn more about Mars' wet past.

articles.title: Bank of England Changes Money Pictures to Animals
articles.summary: The Bank of England is planning to put pictures of British wildlife, like beavers, on its new banknotes instead of famous people such as Sir Winston Churchill and Jane Austen. This decision has made some politicians upset because they think it is silly and erases history. The Bank says it is doing this to make the money harder to copy and because the public wants it. Groups like the RSPCA are suggesting animals like pigeons and rats for the notes. A poll shows that many people support having nature on the notes, but others prefer historical figures. The Bank has not yet chosen which animals will appear.

articles.title: A TV Show About Love and Money for Disabled People
articles.summary: Kyla Harris is an actress who made a TV show called We Might Regret This. She has a boyfriend, but they don't live together because she might lose money from the government. This money helps her pay for things she needs because she is disabled. In the show, a character named Freya, who uses a wheelchair, faces the same problem with her boyfriend. The government gives money to disabled people based on their income, and if they live with a partner, they might get less. This makes it hard for disabled people to choose between love and having enough money. Kyla wants people to talk about this issue more because many don't think disabled people can be in relationships. A charity says disabled people need extra money each month to live like others. The government says there are other benefits that don't depend on income, but the rules can be confusing.

articles.title: The Big Movie Awards Night: Oscars 2026
articles.summary: The Oscars are a big awards show for movies, happening on Sunday night. It's a very exciting year because some awards are hard to predict. The movie 'One Battle After Another' might win the best picture award, but 'Sinners' is also a strong competitor with many nominations. Jessie Buckley is expected to win best actress for her role in 'Hamnet'. Other actors like Michael B Jordan and Timothée Chalamet are also in the running. The show will be on TV in the UK and US, starting at different times. Conan O'Brien is the host, and many famous stars will give out awards. People are watching to see which movies and actors win the golden statues.
